methods.description: Planispiral nanicellids are common in the Givetian to Frasnian bank-to-reef complex of the Holy Cross Mountains (HCM) in central Poland. Isolated specimens of Nanicella were derived from samples collected from marly-shale intercalations of the stromatoporoid-coral limestones of the Kowala railroad cut section. Foraminifera were extracted from the friable sediments by washing through a 60-μm sieve. The material is stored in the S. J. Thugutt Museum of Geology, University of Warsaw.

steps.description: Isolated specimens of Nanicella were derived from samples collected from marly-shale intercalations of the stromatoporoid-coral limestones of the Kowala railroad cut section (set B sensu Szulczewski 51 ; Kowala Formation; see Racki 52 ).

methods.description: Hand-picked isolated specimens, coated with carbon, were examined with a field emission scanning electron microscope (FESEM) at the Institute of High Pressure Physics (Unipress), Polish Academy of Sciences in Warsaw.

steps.searchable_summary: Select and isolate individual specimens by hand-picking
Apply carbon coating to isolated specimens

steps.description: were examined with a field emission scanning electron microscope (FESEM) at the Institute of High Pressure Physics (Unipress), Polish Academy of Sciences in Warsaw.

methods.description: The chemical composition of three selected nanicellid specimens was investigated using a CAMECA SX 100 electron microprobe (EMP) on uncovered, polished, carbon-coated thin sections at the Polish Geological Institute -National Research Institute in Warsaw, Poland. The following conditions were applied: beam diameter: ~5 μm; accelerating voltage: 15 kV; beam current: 5 nA for calcium, and 20 nA for other elements; number of spot analyses: at least 3 per specimen.

methods.description: Thin sections were also examined using cold-cathode cathodoluminescence microscopy (an optical microscope coupled with a Cambridge Image Technology Ltd. CCL Mk5-2) in the Electron Microprobe Laboratory at the Polish Geological Institute -National Research Institute.
